  1. Japanese Denim

Japanese Denim

Where to Find the Best Japanese Selvedge Denim from Hotel East 21

denim
Author

DJG

Published

October 12, 2025

On this page

  • Your Starting Point: Hotel East 21 Tokyo
  • Top 5 Japanese Denim Brands to Look For

Your Starting Point: Hotel East 21 Tokyo

Location: Toyo, Koto-ku, Tokyo
Nearest Stations: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) - 7 min walk - Nishi-ojima Station (Toei Shinjuku Line) - 10 min walk

Top 5 Japanese Denim Brands to Look For

1. Pure Blue Japan (PBJ)

  • Known for: Slubby, textured indigo with incredible fading
  • Signature: Deep indigo with irregular texture
  • Price: ¥25,000-45,000 ($170-310)
  • Fit style: Regular to slim, Japanese sizing

2. Momotaro

  • Known for: “Peach boy” pink selvedge ID, natural indigo
  • Signature: Going-to-Battle stripes, vintage-inspired
  • Price: ¥20,000-40,000 ($140-275)
  • Fit style: Wide range, true vintage cuts to modern slim

3. Japan Blue / Momotaro (same company)

  • Known for: Excellent value, innovative fabrics
  • Signature: Various denims including “Monster” heavyweight
  • Price: ¥15,000-30,000 ($100-210)
  • Fit style: Modern fits, good for first-time buyers

4. Iron Heart

  • Known for: Ultra-heavy weight (21oz-25oz), bulletproof construction
  • Signature: Built like workwear, incredible durability
  • Price: ¥40,000-60,000 ($275-415)
  • Fit style: Substantial, designed for tough wear

5. Kapital

  • Known for: Artistic, distressed, avant-garde styling
  • Signature: Unique washes, patchwork, vintage vibes
  • Price: ¥30,000-80,000+ ($210-550+)
  • Fit style: Relaxed, artisan aesthetic

Honorable Mentions:

  • Fullcount - Vintage Zimbabwe cotton, smooth texture (¥30,000-50,000)
  • Samurai Jeans - Heavy slubby texture, warrior theme (¥30,000-50,000)
  • Studio D’Artisan - Craftsmanship focused, vintage styling (¥25,000-40,000)
  • Warehouse - Faithful reproductions of vintage Levi’s (¥25,000-45,000)
  • ONI Denim - Secret denim texture, unique weaving (¥25,000-40,000)

Top Shops in Tokyo

Hinoya (Ueno)

The Denim Department Store

Location: Ueno, near Ameyoko Market
Address: 6-15-1 Ueno, Taito-ku, Tokyo
Nearest Station: Ueno Station (multiple lines)

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Nihombashi → Transfer to Ginza Line → Ueno - Time: 25-30 minutes - Alternative: Toyocho → Otemachi → Marunouchi Line → Ueno (similar time) - Distance: About 9km

What Makes It Special: - Largest selection of Japanese denim brands under one roof - 3 floors dedicated to denim and workwear - Expert staff who understand sizing and fits - Can try on multiple brands for comparison - Tax-free shopping with passport

Brands Available: - Pure Blue Japan - Momotaro - Japan Blue - Iron Heart - Samurai - Fullcount - Studio D’Artisan - Warehouse - ONI - Sugar Cane - Denime - Many more

Why Visit: - Best one-stop shop for comparing brands - Can spend 2-3 hours trying everything - Staff speak English - Alteration services available

Tips: - Go on weekday if possible (less crowded) - Allow time to try multiple sizes (Japanese sizing runs small) - Ask staff for fade examples of different brands - Combine with Ueno Park/Museums visit

Hours: 11am-8pm daily

Blue in Green (Nakameguro)

Curated Selection with Expertise

Location: Nakameguro
Nearest Station: Nakameguro Station (Hibiya Line)

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Otemachi → Transfer to Hibiya Line → Nakameguro - Time: 35-40 minutes - Distance: About 12km - Can combine with Traveler’s Factory visit!

What Makes It Special: - Carefully curated selection - Focus on premium brands and quality - Knowledgeable staff passionate about denim - Personal service, not overwhelming - Beautiful shop aesthetic

Brands Available: - Pure Blue Japan - Momotaro - Strike Gold - Real McCoy’s - Flat Head - Exclusive collaborations

Why Visit: - Premium experience - Expert guidance on sizing and care - Smaller selection but all top-tier - 1-2 hours sufficient

Tips: - Call ahead to confirm stock of specific sizes - Be prepared for higher-end pricing - Great if you know what you want - Very knowledgeable about fade patterns

Hours: 12pm-8pm (closed Wednesdays)

Americanaya (Shibuya/Harajuku)

Multiple Locations, Great Selection

Location: Shibuya (main) + Harajuku location
Nearest Station: Shibuya Station

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Otemachi → Transfer to Hanzomon Line → Shibuya - Time: 35-40 minutes - Distance: About 15km

What Makes It Special: - Good selection at reasonable prices - Mix of brands and price points - Tourist-friendly - Multiple locations for convenience

Brands Available: - Momotaro - Japan Blue - Edwin - Lee Japan - Other Japanese and import brands

Why Visit: - Good if you’re already in Shibuya/Harajuku - More approachable than specialty boutiques - Decent selection without being overwhelming

Hours: 11am-8pm daily

Bears (Harajuku)

Americana & Denim Specialist

Location: Harajuku
Nearest Station: Harajuku Station (Yamanote Line)

From Hotel East 21: - Toyocho Station (Tozai Line) → Otemachi → Marunouchi Line → Shinjuku → Yamanote Line to Harajuku - Time: 40-45 minutes - Alternative: Multiple transfer options - Distance: About 14km

What Makes It Special: - Americana workwear focus - Vintage-inspired Japanese brands - Well-curated selection - Cool vintage shop vibe

Brands Available: - Warehouse - Fullcount - Trophy Clothing - Buzz Rickson’s - Studio D’Artisan

Why Visit: - If you love vintage Americana aesthetic - Quality over quantity - Good staff knowledge - Can combine with Harajuku shopping

Hours: 12pm-8pm (closed Wednesdays)

Okura (Shinjuku)

Hidden Gem in Shinjuku

Location: Shinjuku
Nearest Station: Shinjuku-sanchome Station

From Hotel East 21: - Nishi-ojima Station (Toei Shinjuku Line) → Direct to Shinjuku-sanchome (no transfers!) - Time: 25-30 minutes - Distance: About 12km - EASIEST ROUTE - direct line!

What Makes It Special: - Less touristy than Shibuya/Harajuku shops - Solid selection of major brands - Good prices - Local vibe

Brands Available: - Momotaro - Japan Blue - Edwin - Iron Heart - Various other Japanese brands

Why Visit: - Convenient direct train from hotel - Can combine with Shinjuku shopping/sightseeing - Less overwhelming than Hinoya - Good balance of selection and browsability

Hours: 11am-8pm daily

Shopping Districts

Ueno/Ameyoko Area

Best for: One-stop shopping, variety, value
Distance from hotel: 25-30 min
Vibe: Market-like, bustling, lots of options

Shops Here: - Hinoya (main destination) - Various smaller denim/workwear shops - Vintage stores

Harajuku/Shibuya

Best for: Trendy, young vibe, multiple shops within walking distance
Distance from hotel: 35-45 min
Vibe: Fashion-forward, energetic

Shops Here: - Americanaya - Bears - Multiple smaller boutiques - Streetwear shops

Nakameguro/Daikanyama

Best for: Curated, upscale, artisan
Distance from hotel: 35-40 min
Vibe: Trendy, upscale, design-focused

Shops Here: - Blue in Green - Various boutiques - Lifestyle shops

Shinjuku

Best for: Convenience, good selection, easy from hotel
Distance from hotel: 25-30 min direct
Vibe: Urban, commercial, accessible

Shops Here: - Okura - Department stores with denim sections

Price Guide

Budget Friendly (for Japanese denim)

¥15,000-25,000 ($100-170) - Japan Blue entry level - Edwin made-in-Japan - Some Momotaro models - Lee Japan

Mid-Range

¥25,000-40,000 ($170-275) - Most Pure Blue Japan - Most Momotaro - Fullcount - Samurai - Studio D’Artisan - Warehouse

Premium

¥40,000-60,000+ ($275-415+) - Iron Heart - Kapital - Real McCoy’s - Limited editions - Collaboration pieces

What You Get for Your Money

Entry Level (¥15,000-25,000): - Good quality selvedge denim - Japanese construction - Will fade nicely with wear - Good starting point

Mid-Range (¥25,000-40,000): - Premium Japanese denim fabric - Artisan construction details - Unique textures and indigo - Best fade potential - Sweet spot for enthusiasts

Premium (¥40,000+): - Ultra-heavy fabrics - Special dyeing techniques - Limited production runs - Heirloom quality - Investment pieces

Sizing Guide

Important: Japanese Sizing

Japanese sizes run 1-2 sizes smaller than US/EU: - US 32 = Japan 34 or 36 - US 34 = Japan 36 or 38 - Measure your best-fitting jeans and compare - ALWAYS try on before buying

Fit Styles

Regular/Vintage: - Higher rise - Roomier thigh - Straight or slight taper - Authentic vintage proportions

Slim/Modern: - Lower rise - Tapered leg - Contemporary fit - More fashion-forward

Relaxed: - Comfortable fit - Room in seat and thigh - Workwear heritage

Key Measurements

Bring these measurements (in inches or cm): - Waist (where you wear pants) - Inseam (length you need) - Thigh circumference - Leg opening

Pro tip: Most shops have measuring tapes and will help you find the right size.

Shopping Strategy from Hotel East 21

Half Day - Best Bang for Buck

Ueno Denim Day: - Morning: Train to Ueno (25-30 min) - 2-3 hours at Hinoya - Try everything, compare brands - Lunch at Ameyoko Market - Browse Ueno Park if time - Back to hotel

Total time: 4-5 hours
This is your BEST option for selection and value

Easy Afternoon - Convenient

Shinjuku Quick Trip: - Direct train to Shinjuku (25-30 min, no transfers!) - Okura + Tokyu Hands (stationery shopping!) - Explore Shinjuku - Back to hotel

Total time: 3-4 hours
Most convenient from your hotel

Full Day - Premium Experience

Nakameguro + Shibuya: - Morning: Traveler’s Factory + Blue in Green (Nakameguro) - Lunch in Nakameguro - Afternoon: Train to Shibuya - Americanaya + Shibuya shopping - Evening: Back to hotel

Total time: 6-8 hours
Best if you want multiple experiences

Full Day - Comprehensive

Ueno + Harajuku: - Morning: Hinoya (Ueno) - 2-3 hours - Lunch in Ueno - Train to Harajuku (30 min) - Bears + Harajuku shopping - Back to hotel

Total time: 6-8 hours
Maximum brand exposure

Quick Reference: Denim Shop Distance from Hotel East 21

Shop Time Transfers Direct Line Priority
Okura (Shinjuku) 25-30 min 0 Toei Shinjuku ⭐ Easiest
Hinoya (Ueno) 25-30 min 1 Tozai → Ginza/Marunouchi ⭐⭐⭐ Best Selection
Blue in Green 35-40 min 1 Tozai → Hibiya ⭐⭐ Premium
Americanaya (Shibuya) 35-40 min 1-2 Tozai → Hanzomon ⭐ Tourist-friendly
Bears (Harajuku) 40-45 min 2 Multiple transfers ⭐ Vintage focus

What to Expect

The Experience

At Hinoya: - Browse 3 floors - Try on multiple brands back-to-back - Staff will bring different sizes - Can compare fade patterns - Plan 2-3 hours minimum

At Boutiques: - More personal service - Smaller selection but curated - Expert advice on fit and care - 1-2 hours sufficient

Buying Process

  1. Browse - Look at different brands and styles
  2. Measure - Staff will measure you or help you measure
  3. Try On - Try multiple sizes (sizing varies by brand)
  4. Check Fit - Staff will advise on proper fit (should be snug, will stretch)
  5. Choose - Pick your favorite
  6. Tax-Free - Present passport for tax exemption (saves ~10%)
  7. Alteration - Hemming available (may take a day or can ship to hotel)

Payment

  • Credit cards accepted at major shops
  • Cash preferred at smaller boutiques
  • Tax-free requires passport and minimum purchase (usually ¥5,000+)

Care & Breaking In

What Staff Will Tell You

First Wear: - Don’t wash for at least 6 months (or longer) - The longer you wait, the better the fades - Some people wait a year or more

Breaking In: - Will be stiff at first - Fabric will soften and conform to your body - Be patient through the first few weeks

First Wash: - Turn inside out - Cold water - No detergent (or very mild) - Hang dry - Never machine dry

Philosophy: - The jeans are an investment - They tell your story through fading - The “best” jeans are worn hard and washed little - Embrace the process

Tips for Success

Before You Go

Research: - Look at fade photos online (search “[brand name] fade”) - Understand different denim weights (12oz-25oz) - Know what style you want (vintage, modern, workwear)

Bring: - Passport (for tax-free) - Measurements of your best-fitting jeans - Extra cash - Patience for trying things on

At the Shop

Do: - Try on multiple sizes - Ask about fade characteristics - Ask to see fade examples - Take your time - Ask questions about care

Don’t: - Rush the decision - Buy based only on tagged size (Japanese sizing varies) - Be afraid to go heavier weight than expected - Forget about shrinkage (raw denim shrinks 1-2 inches in waist/length)

Language

  • Most shops have English-speaking staff
  • Denim terms are often universal
  • Point to examples if needed
  • Staff are used to international customers

Combining Denim Shopping with Sightseeing

Ueno Day

  • Morning: Hinoya denim shopping
  • Lunch: Ameyoko Market street food
  • Afternoon: Ueno Park, Museums, Temples
  • Evening: Return to hotel

Shinjuku Day

  • Morning: Okura denim + Tokyu Hands stationery
  • Lunch: Shinjuku restaurant district
  • Afternoon: Tokyo Metropolitan Building observation deck (free views!)
  • Evening: Kabukicho entertainment district

Nakameguro/Shibuya Day

  • Morning: Traveler’s Factory + Blue in Green denim
  • Lunch: Nakameguro cafes
  • Afternoon: Walk to Daikanyama, then Shibuya
  • Evening: Shibuya crossing, shopping, nightlife

Harajuku Day

  • Morning: Meiji Shrine
  • Lunch: Harajuku crepes, street food
  • Afternoon: Bears denim + Harajuku fashion shopping
  • Evening: Omotesando architecture walk

Budget Planning

Sample Purchase Scenarios

Entry Level: - 1 pair Japan Blue or entry Momotaro: ¥20,000 ($140) - Tax-free savings: ¥2,000 ($14) - Total: ¥18,000 ($125)

Enthusiast: - 1 pair Pure Blue Japan: ¥35,000 ($240) - 1 pair Momotaro: ¥25,000 ($170) - Tax-free savings: ¥6,000 ($42) - Total: ¥54,000 ($375)

Collector: - 1 pair Iron Heart: ¥50,000 ($345) - 1 pair Pure Blue Japan: ¥35,000 ($240) - 1 pair Kapital: ¥40,000 ($275) - Tax-free savings: ¥12,500 ($87) - Total: ¥112,500 ($780)

Additional Costs

Hemming/Alterations: - Chain stitch hemming: ¥2,000-3,000 ($14-21) - May take 1-3 days - Some shops include free hemming

Shipping: - Ship to hotel: Usually free - Ship international: ¥3,000-8,000 depending on service

Why Japanese Denim?

What Makes It Special

Craftsmanship: - Made on vintage shuttle looms - Attention to every detail - Artisan approach to production - Small-batch manufacturing

Quality: - Premium long-staple cotton - Natural indigo dyeing (some brands) - Selvedge construction - Will last decades

Fading: - Creates unique wear patterns - Tells your story - Each pair becomes one-of-a-kind - “Earned” patina

Heritage: - Japanese companies perfected American denim - Combined US heritage with Japanese precision - Now considered the world’s best denim

Final Recommendations

If You Can Only Visit ONE Shop:

Hinoya (Ueno) - Best selection, can compare everything, 25-30 min from hotel

If You Want Easy Convenience:

Okura (Shinjuku) - Direct train (no transfers!), good selection, can combine with Tokyu Hands

If You Want Premium Experience:

Blue in Green (Nakameguro) - Curated, expert service, can combine with Traveler’s Factory

If You’re Serious About Denim:

Visit 2-3 shops - Hinoya for variety, Blue in Green for premium, maybe Americanaya or Bears for different perspective

Bottom Line: Japanese denim is an investment in quality and craftsmanship. Take your time, try things on, ask questions, and enjoy the experience. Your hotel location gives you good access to the best shops, with Ueno’s Hinoya being your top priority and Shinjuku’s Okura being your most convenient option.

Wear them hard, wash them rarely, and watch them become your favorite jeans.

Korea & Japan Trip 2025
